title: Frangula neblinensis
taxonomy: Frangula neblinensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. It belongs to the genus Frangula and is closely related to other species like Frangula alnus and Frangula purshiana. Frangula neblinensis is native to South America, specifically in the Andean region. The plant is classified as an evergreen shrub or small tree, known for its distinctive leaves and small, yellowish-green flowers. The classification of Frangula neblinensis is still being studied, and more research is needed to fully understand its classification and relationships within the plant kingdom.
uses: Frangula neblinensis has been reported to have potential medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
